course_description: Giao tiếp công sở: small talk, email chuyên nghiệp, điện thoại, họp & ý kiến, modals & xin lỗi, cơ cấu công ty, báo cáo tiến độ (present perfect), lịch hẹn, thuyết trình, văn hóa làm việc từ xa.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Formal vs informal
theory_pages.body:
### Formal vs informal greetings

**Formal** (sếp, khách, đối tác lần đầu):

- *Good morning / Good afternoon.*  
- *How do you do?* (rất trang trọng, lần đầu gặp)  
- *It's a pleasure to meet you.*

**Informal** (đồng nghiệp thân):

- *Morning!* / *How's it going?* / *What's up?*

> Trong môi trường chuyên nghiệp, chào **quá thân mật** với lãnh đạo/đối tác có thể được đánh giá là **không phù hợp** (fail ngữ cảnh).

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Safe small talk
theory_pages.body:
### Safe small talk (văn phòng)

- **Thời tiết:** *Terrible weather today, isn't it?*  
- **Cuối tuần:** *How was your weekend?* / *Did you do anything fun?*  
- **Công việc (nhẹ):** *Are you busy today?* / *How is the new project going?*

Tránh: lương, chính trị, chủ đề nhạy cảm cá nhân (trừ khi đã rất thân).

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Kết thúc hội thoại
theory_pages.body:
### Ending a chat politely

Không **im lặng bỏ đi**. Dùng:

- *Well, I should get back to work.*  
- *It was great talking to you, but I have a meeting in 5 minutes.*

### Thực hành

Viết **2 lời thoại ngắn**: một formal (chào đối tác), một informal (chào bạn cùng phòng).

content.reading_passage: Good morning, Mr. Lee. It's a pleasure to meet you. — Good morning. Terrible weather today, isn't it? Well, I should get back to work. Nice talking to you.

vocabulary.word: How do you do? / pleasure to meet you

examples.vietnamese: Cuối tuần của bạn thế nào?

quizzes.question_text: Câu nào phù hợp nhất để chào đối tác lần đầu gặp mặt?

options.B: It's a pleasure to meet you.

quizzes.question_text: Chủ đề small talk tương đối an toàn ở văn phòng:
quizzes.hint_text: Weather is a classic safe topic.

quizzes.question_text: Well, I should get back to _____.
Sửa: “How was your weekends?”
Ghép: 1. Formal — 2. Informal
Cách kết thúc cuộc trò chuyện lịch sự:

options.B: I have a meeting in 5 minutes.

quizzes.question_text: You should greet your boss with “What's up?” in most offices.
“Cuối tuần của bạn thế nào?”
If someone asks “How's it going?”, a natural reply is:
Terrible weather today, _____ it?

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Openings & closings
theory_pages.body:
### Openings & closings

**Mở:** *Dear Mr. Smith,* (formal) / *Hi Team,* (nội bộ).

**Đóng:** *Sincerely,* (rất formal) / *Best regards,* / *Kind regards,* (rất phổ biến).

Luôn **viết hoa** chữ cái đầu: *Dear*, *Best regards* — không viết *hi boss*.

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Purpose
theory_pages.body:
### Stating the purpose

Đi **thẳng vào việc** — không vòng vo.

**I am writing to + verb…**

- *I am writing to inform you about the meeting.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Attachments & CTA
theory_pages.body:
### Attachments & calls to action

- *Please find attached the report.* / *I have attached the document.*  
- *Please let me know if you have any questions.*  
- *I look forward to hearing from you.*

content.reading_passage:
Dear Sales team,

I am writing to inform you about the new schedule. Please find attached the report.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Best regards,
Anna

vocabulary.vi: kết thư trang trọng thân mật vừa phải

examples.english: I look forward to hearing from you.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi mong nhận được phản hồi từ bạn.

quizzes.question_text: Cách kết email phổ biến và an toàn trong kinh doanh:
I am _____ to inform you about the new schedule.
Sửa: “Please find attach the file.”
Khi đính kèm báo cáo, cách viết tốt nhất:

options.B: The report is in the email body only wrong

quizzes.question_text: Ghép: 1. Opening — 2. Closing
quizzes.hint_text: Dear… at start; Sincerely at end.

quizzes.question_text: You should write “HI BOSS” at the start of a professional email.
“Tôi mong nhận được phản hồi từ bạn.”

options.A: I look forward to hearing from you.
options.B: I look forward to hear you.
options.C: I forward look to hearing you.
options.D: I am forward to hear you.

quizzes.hint_text: to + V-ing after look forward to.

quizzes.question_text: After sending a complex document, a polite follow-up line is:

options.A: Please let me know if you have any questions.

quizzes.question_text: Dear _____ team, (Kính gửi nhóm Bán hàng)
Viết hoa đúng chuẩn:
quizzes.hint_text: Best regards, (only B capitalized in closing line).

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Answering & asking
theory_pages.body:
### Answering & asking for someone

**Người nhận:** *Hello, [Company name], [Your name] speaking. How can I help you?*

**Người gọi:** *Hi, this is [name] from [company]. Could I speak to Mr. Davis, please?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Unavailable
theory_pages.body:
### When someone is unavailable

- *I'm afraid he is in a meeting.*  
- *She is not at her desk right now.*

**Đề xuất:** *Can I take a message?* / *Would you like to leave a message?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Messages
theory_pages.body:
### Leaving a message

- *Yes, please tell him that Anna called.*  
- *Could you ask him to call me back at [number]?*

content.reading_passage: Hello, Bright Tech, Linh speaking. How can I help you? — Hi, this is Tom from Delta Ltd. Could I speak to Ms. Park, please? — I'm afraid she is in a meeting. Can I take a message?

vocabulary.vi: đang nghe máy (đầu dây bên này là…)

examples.vietnamese: Tôi có thể nhận lời nhắn không?

quizzes.question_text: Bốc máy công ty, bạn nói:
Could I _____ to Ms. Sarah, please?
Cách lịch sự khi sếp đang bận:

options.B: I'm afraid he is in a meeting.

quizzes.question_text: Sửa: “Can I leave a messages?”
On the phone, “This is Anna” often replaces “I am Anna.”
Ghép: 1. Caller — 2. Receiver

options.A: 1→Could you ask him to call me back? — 2→Can I take a message?
options.B: 1→Can I take a message? — 2→Could you ask him to call me back?

quizzes.hint_text: Caller asks callback; receiver offers message.

quizzes.question_text: “Tôi có thể nhận lời nhắn không?”

options.C: Can I leave a message to you wrong

quizzes.question_text: Please _____ her that David called.
A client reads a phone number very fast. You say:
“Unavailable” on the phone usually means:

options.B: Not free / not able to take the call

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Opinions
theory_pages.body:
### Giving opinions

Đừng chỉ lặp *I think…* — đa dạng hóa:

- *In my opinion, …*  
- *From my point of view, …*  
- *I strongly believe that …*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Agreeing
theory_pages.body:
### Agreeing

- *I completely agree.*  
- *That's a valid point.*  
- *I feel exactly the same way.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Disagreeing politely
theory_pages.body:
### Disagreeing politely

Tránh *You are wrong* trong họp.

**Khen trước, phản biện sau:** *I see your point, but …*

- *I'm afraid I have to disagree.*  
- *That's a good idea, but how about …?*

content.reading_passage: In my opinion, we should delay the launch. That's a valid point about the budget. I see your point, but the risks are high. I'm afraid I have to disagree with the timeline.

vocabulary.vi: ý hợp lý / phản đối lịch sự

examples.english: I'm afraid I have to disagree.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi e là tôi phải không đồng ý.

quizzes.question_text: Mở đầu để đưa ý kiến cá nhân:
I completely _____. (đồng ý)
Cách phản biện lịch sự:
Sửa: “From my point of views…”
Ghép: 1. Agree — 2. Disagree

options.A: 1→That's a valid point — 2→I'm afraid I have to disagree
options.B: 1→I'm afraid I have to disagree — 2→That's a valid point

quizzes.hint_text: Valid point = agree tone; disagree phrase for opposition.

quizzes.question_text: I strongly _____ that we should launch the product now.
Using only “I think…” repeatedly is the best style in every meeting.
“Tôi e là tôi phải không đồng ý.”

options.A: I'm afraid I have to disagree.

quizzes.question_text: Your boss suggests a risky idea. You respond professionally:

options.B: That's an interesting idea, but what about the risks?

quizzes.question_text: “Valid point” means:

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Polite requests
theory_pages.body:
### Polite requests (modals)

Tránh mệnh lệnh: *Send me the file!*

- *Could you + verb… please?* — *Could you send me the report, please?*  
- *Would you mind + V-ing?* — *Would you mind sending me the report?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Permission
theory_pages.body:
### Asking permission

- *Do you mind if I leave early today?*

**Đồng ý:** *No, not at all.* / *Go ahead.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Apologies
theory_pages.body:
### Professional apologies

- *I apologize for the delay.*  
- *I'm sorry for the inconvenience — I will fix it right away.*

content.reading_passage: Could you review this draft, please? Would you mind checking the numbers? Do you mind if I leave at 4 PM? I apologize for the late reply.

examples.vietnamese: Tôi xin lỗi vì sự chậm trễ.

quizzes.question_text: Cách yêu cầu gửi file lịch sự nhất:

options.B: Could you send me the file, please?

quizzes.question_text: Would you mind _____ (help) me with this project?
Sửa: “I apologize of the mistake.”
“Do you mind if I open the window?” — To agree, you say:
quizzes.hint_text: No, not at all = not bothered.

quizzes.question_text: “Tôi xin lỗi vì sự chậm trễ.”
After “Would you mind”, the verb is usually in -ing form.
I am sorry for the _____ (inconvenience).
A customer complains about a system error. You should say:

options.B: I apologize for the inconvenience. We are fixing it.

quizzes.question_text: “Apologize” is closest to:
Sắp xếp đúng:

options.A: Would you mind closing the door?
options.B: Would you mind close the door?
options.C: Would you mind to closing the door?

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Departments
theory_pages.body:
### Core departments

- **HR** — Human Resources (nhân sự)  
- **IT** — Information Technology  
- **Sales & Marketing**  
- **Accounting / Finance**  
- **R&D** — Research and Development

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Titles
theory_pages.body:
### Titles & roles

*CEO*, *manager*, *supervisor*, *employee / staff*, *colleague / coworker*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Responsibilities
theory_pages.body:
### Responsibilities

- *I am responsible for + V-ing / noun* — *I am responsible for training new staff.*  
- *I am in charge of …* — *I am in charge of the marketing budget.*

content.reading_passage: Our HR department hires new employees. IT fixes computers. I am responsible for monthly reports. My manager is in charge of the whole team.

examples.english: I am in charge of the IT department.

quizzes.question_text: Phòng lo tuyển dụng nhân sự:
I am responsible _____ creating monthly reports.
Sửa: “I am in charge for the project.”
Người quản lý trực tiếp thường gọi là:
Ghép: 1. Accounting — 2. R&D
A person you work with is your _____.
CEO stands for Chief Employee Officer.
“Tôi phụ trách phòng IT.”

options.A: I am in charge of the IT department.
options.B: I am in charge for the IT department.

quizzes.question_text: If your computer breaks, you usually contact:
After “responsible for”, we often use:

lessons.lesson_name: Reporting Progress (Present Perfect vs Past Simple)

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Present perfect
theory_pages.body:
### Present perfect (reporting)

*Have/has + past participle* — kết quả / kinh nghiệm liên quan **hiện tại**, báo cáo tiến độ.

*I have finished the report.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Just, already, yet
theory_pages.body:
### Just / already / yet

- *I have just sent the email.*  
- *We have already booked the flight.*  
- *I haven't received the data yet.* (yet ở cuối câu phủ định/câu hỏi)

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — vs Past simple
theory_pages.body:
### vs Past simple

Có **mốc thời gian cụ thể** trong quá khứ (*yesterday*, *last week*) → **past simple**.

✓ *I sent the report yesterday.*  
✗ *I have sent the report yesterday.*

content.reading_passage: I have finished the slides. I have just emailed the client. We met the client last week. I haven't received their feedback yet.

vocabulary.vi: đã / chưa / vừa mới (với present perfect)

examples.english: I have just called the client.
examples.vietnamese: Tôi vừa mới gọi cho khách hàng.

quizzes.question_text: Boss: “Have you finished the task?” You answer:

options.B: Yes, I have already finished it.

quizzes.question_text: I haven't completed the presentation _____.
Which part is wrong: “We have met the client last week.”
quizzes.hint_text: We met the client last week (past simple).

quizzes.question_text: I _____ just updated the software.
“Tôi vừa mới gọi cho khách hàng.”

options.A: I have just called the client.
options.B: I just have called the client.
options.C: I have called just the client.
options.D: I called just have the client.

quizzes.question_text: We often use “yet” at the end of a negative sentence to mean “not until now”.
You submitted the report at 8 AM; now it is 10 AM. You say:

options.B: I have sent it at 8 AM.
options.C: I have sent it since 8 AM wrong

quizzes.question_text: Ghép: 1. Already — 2. Yet
Sắp xếp đúng:

options.A: I have already sent the email.
options.B: I already have sent the email.
options.C: I sent have already the email.
options.D: I have sent already the email.

quizzes.question_text: The IT team _____ (fix) the bug. It is working now.

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Proposing time
theory_pages.body:
### Proposing a time

- *Are you available on Tuesday at 3 PM?*  
- *Can we set up a meeting for next week?*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Postpone & cancel
theory_pages.body:
### Postpone / cancel / move

- *Can we postpone the meeting to Friday?* / *push it back*  
- *Can we bring it forward to Monday?* (sớm hơn)  
- *I'm afraid I have to cancel our appointment.*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Confirming
theory_pages.body:
### Confirming

- *Tuesday at 3 PM works for me.*  
- *I will send you a calendar invite.*

content.reading_passage: Are you available on Monday at 10? Can we set up a call? If not, we can push the meeting back to Wednesday. Tuesday works for me.

vocabulary.vi: thời gian đó phù hợp với tôi

examples.english: Can we postpone the meeting to Friday?
examples.vietnamese: Chúng ta có thể hoãn họp sang thứ Sáu không?

quizzes.question_text: To ask if someone is free, you often use:
Can we set _____ a meeting for tomorrow?
Sửa: “Tuesday works to me.”
A synonym for “postpone” in scheduling:
“Chúng ta có thể dời buổi họp sang thứ Sáu không?”

options.A: Can we postpone the meeting to Friday?
options.B: Can we forward the meeting to Friday?
options.C: Can we the meeting postpone Friday?

quizzes.question_text: “Bring forward” means to schedule earlier.
I will send you a calendar _____. (common in Outlook/Google)
Reply to “Are you available on Monday?”
Ghép: 1. Cancel — 2. Propose a slot
“That time works for me” means:

options.A: That time suits me / is OK

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Introduction
theory_pages.body:
### Opening a presentation

- *Good morning everyone. Thank you for coming.*  
- *Today, I am going to talk about …*  
- *My presentation is divided into three parts. First, … Second, … Finally, …*

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Signposting
theory_pages.body:
### Signposting (moving between points)

- *Let's move on to the next point.*  
- *Turning our attention to the budget …*  
- *As you can see on this slide …*

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Q&A
theory_pages.body:
### Q&A

- *That brings me to the end of my presentation.*  
- *I would be happy to answer any questions.*  
- *That's a good question. Let me check and get back to you later.*

content.reading_passage: Good morning everyone. Today I am going to talk about sales. First, I will outline the goals. Let's move on to the next point. As you can see on this slide, revenue grew. That brings me to the end of my presentation. I would be happy to answer any questions.

vocabulary.vi: chỉ dẫn người nghe / chuyển sang ý tiếp

examples.english: Let's move on to the budget.
examples.vietnamese: Hãy chuyển sang phần ngân sách.

quizzes.question_text: To start a presentation professionally:

options.B: Today, I am going to talk about…

quizzes.question_text: My presentation is divided _____ three parts.
Sửa: “Let's move in to the next slide.”
To direct attention to the slide:

options.B: As you can see on this slide…

quizzes.question_text: “Hãy chuyển sang phần ngân sách.”

options.A: Let's move on to the budget.
options.B: Let's move in to the budget.

quizzes.question_text: In Q&A, saying only “I don't know” is the most professional option.
That brings me to the _____ of my presentation.
Sắp xếp đúng:

options.A: I would be happy to answer any questions.
options.B: I would happy be to answer any questions.
options.C: I happy would answer any questions.
options.D: Would I be happy answer any questions.

quizzes.question_text: Signposting helps the audience:

options.A: Follow the structure of your talk

quizzes.question_text: “Signposting” in presentations means:

options.A: Using language to guide listeners through sections

theory_pages.title: Trang 1 — Vocabulary
theory_pages.body:
### Pre-reading

*Remote work / telecommuting*, *hybrid model*, *flexible hours*, *work-life balance*.

theory_pages.title: Trang 2 — Bài đọc
theory_pages.body:
### Reading

Đọc về **hybrid & remote** — lợi ích và **thách thức giao tiếp**.

theory_pages.title: Trang 3 — Phân tích
theory_pages.body:
### Post-reading — link to this course

Lợi ích: linh hoạt, cân bằng. Thách thức: không gặp trực tiếp → cần **email, tin nhắn, họp video** rõ ràng — ôn lại các bài trước (email, small talk, lịch hẹn).

content.reading_passage: In recent years, the traditional office has changed. Many companies now use a hybrid model. Employees work from home for three days and go to the office for two days a week. This model gives staff flexible hours and improves their work-life balance. However, remote work has challenges. Communication can be difficult. Colleagues can't just walk to another desk to ask a quick question. Instead, they have to schedule video calls on Zoom or send messages on Slack. To succeed in a remote team, clear and professional written communication is essential.

vocabulary.vi: làm việc kết hợp / từ xa
cân bằng công việc và cuộc sống

quizzes.question_text: What is a “hybrid model” in the text?

options.B: Working both at home and in the office

quizzes.question_text: Remote work has no challenges.
The hybrid model improves an employee's work-life _____.
Why is communication difficult in remote work (according to the text)?

options.A: You can't just walk to someone's desk.

quizzes.question_text: Tools mentioned for communication include Zoom and _____.
What is “essential” for remote teams?
A synonym for “staff” in the passage:
Flexible hours mean you must work exactly 9–5 with no variation.
“Làm việc từ xa có những thử thách.”
The text discusses pros and cons of the modern _____.
